title: Local chat assistant, using your GPU
summary: Your computer has enough GPU to run a 7-14B model at good speed. You can have a full assistant without sending anything to any server.

steps.why: Install Ollama and download a quantized 7-8B model (e.g. Llama 3.1 8B or Qwen3 8B) — with your VRAM you'll run it smoothly.
Add Open WebUI on top of Ollama to get a comfortable chat interface with conversation history, instead of using only the terminal.

title: Local chat assistant, without a powerful GPU
summary: Without a GPU with enough VRAM, a local model is still possible, but more limited and slower — fine for occasional tasks, not for heavy use.

steps.why: Install Ollama and use a small model (3B or under) quantized to 4 bits. It will run on CPU, slower than with a GPU but functional.

title: Cloud chat assistant, free
summary: No cost, no install: use a cloud provider's free tier. It has usage limits, but it's the fastest way to get started.

steps.why: Create an account and use the free tier. Try Claude and others too — there's no single "best" one, each has different strengths.

title: Cloud chat assistant, paid plan
summary: With budget for a paid plan, you get more capacity, higher limits and access to each provider's most capable models.

steps.why: The paid plan raises the usage limit and gives access to the provider's most capable models.

title: Local coding assistant
summary: With enough GPU, you can have code completion and code chat without your code ever leaving your computer.

steps.why: Download a code-specialized model (e.g. Qwen2.5-Coder) with Ollama.
Install the Continue extension in your editor and connect it to your local Ollama — you'll get code completion and chat without leaving the editor.

title: Cloud coding assistant
summary: No special hardware needed: a coding assistant built into your editor, with a free tier to get started.

steps.why: Install the extension in your editor and start with the free plan (about 2000 completions/month).

title: Local image generation
summary: With enough GPU, you can generate unlimited images at no per-generation cost, with full control over the model.

steps.why: Install ComfyUI and download a Stable Diffusion (or similar) model that fits your VRAM.

title: Cloud image generation
summary: Nothing to install, no GPU needed: generate images from a cloud service, with a free tier to get started.

steps.why: ChatGPT includes image generation right inside the conversation, on both the free and paid tiers.

title: Chat with your documents, locally
summary: Upload your files to a tool that runs on your computer — neither the documents nor the questions leave your machine.

steps.why: AnythingLLM bundles a model + vector database + interface into a single desktop app — upload your documents and ask it directly.

title: Chat with your documents, in the cloud
summary: The fastest way to start: upload files directly to a cloud chatbot and ask about them.

steps.why: Upload your documents directly in the conversation (PDF, Word, etc.) and ask about their content.

title: Automate tasks with an agent
summary: Connect several tools together (email, spreadsheets, APIs) with flows that trigger on their own, with or without an AI step in the middle.

steps.why: Start self-hosted (free, via Docker) if you want full control, or on n8n Cloud if you'd rather not install anything.

title: A safe starting point
summary: We couldn't find a specific route for your exact combination of answers (possibly because your hardware or budget isn't enough for the local routes for this goal). This is the safest starting point in the meantime: free, nothing to install, and you can switch routes as soon as your hardware, budget or level change.

steps.why: Start with the free tier of a cloud assistant — no install, no special hardware needed.
